@graph.name: forrt-replication-zarr-consistency
@graph.description: Fault-injection replication testing whether Icechunk-backed transactional Zarr stores eliminate the metadata-data inconsistencies that affect disconnected STAC-style metadata indexes over object storage, including a positive control for the conditional-write/CAS guarantee on a real S3-compatible object store (NIRD/Sigma2).

@graph.name: Replication pipeline
@graph.description: Snakemake workflow running the fault-injection harness (no external dataset — synthetic Zarr arrays are generated and faults injected against Icechunk- and STAC-backed stores) and rendering the comparison figure.

@graph.name: Pixi workspace manifest (declared dependencies, features, tasks)
Pixi per-platform lockfile (every package pinned to an exact build hash)
01 — Atomic synchronization fault-injection matrix and figure
@graph.description: Runs the F1-F4 fault matrix across Icechunk and STAC-style backends (via harness.run_matrix.run_all), writes data/results/results.parquet, and renders figures/main_result.png.

@graph.name: Fault-injection harness
@graph.description: Python package implementing the F1-F4 fault scenarios (faults.py), the Icechunk and STAC baseline backends (baseline_icechunk.py, baseline_stac.py, backends.py), the consistency invariant checker (invariant.py), and the matrix runner (run_matrix.py). Invoked from notebooks/01_atomic_sync.py.

@graph.name: Source paper PDF directory (not used — this is a question-rooted chain; see paper/README.md)
Generated results (gitignored)
@graph.description: Results table (data/results/results.parquet) produced by the fault-injection harness. There is no external dataset to download — see data/README.md for why — and these files are never committed (.gitignore keeps only data/README.md and data/.gitkeep).
